steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The text opens with a generalization: I have always noticed that it is the fixed idea of each member of the crew that he is doing everything. It then offers specific examples: Harris thinks only he has worked; George thinks only he himself has done all the labor.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content:
Choice D describes exactly that: a generalization followed by supporting examples from a specific situation (the boat trip).

A is wrong: nothing in the text presents the situation as puzzling — the narrator says he has always noticed it. B is wrong: there's no conflict being resolved. C is wrong: the narrator never claims to know his own role; he describes the others' overconfidence.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
